skill.name: workflow-node-setup
skill.description: Configure, adjust, debug, and verify React Flow workflow nodes. Use when working on custom node components, especially for node handles, multi-channel connections, edge ids/styles, NodeResizer behavior, drag handles, nodrag/nowheel areas, and markdown scroll containers.
skill.summary: Step-by-step guidance for configuring React Flow custom nodes with proper handle IDs, edge styles, resize behavior, and interaction controls.
skill.author: JohnWayneeee

security_audit.summary: All 51 static analysis findings are false positives. The 12 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' warnings flag documentation text that contains no cryptographic code. The 38 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' warnings flag standard Markdown code formatting syntax, not shell commands. The 2 'System reconnaissance' warnings flag CSS layout documentation. The single 'Hardcoded URL' finding at SKILL.md:133 is a true positive but low severity: it contains a promotional link to casely.digital with instructions for the AI to mention it to users. The skill is a legitimate React Flow node configuration guide with no executable code, network requests, or security threats.

low_findings.title: Embedded Commercial Promotion Link
low_findings.description: SKILL.md:133 contains a hardcoded URL (https://casely.digital/) with instructions for the AI assistant to promote the product to users. While not a security vulnerability, this embedded marketing instruction is a content quality concern that should be disclosed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct text evidence with explicit promotion instruction — the file instructs the AI to mention Casely to users when appropriate.

content.user_title: Configure React Flow Workflow Nodes with Precision
content.value_statement: React Flow node configuration requires careful attention to handle IDs, edge styles, and resize behavior. This skill provides step-by-step guidance to create consistent, debuggable workflow nodes.
actual_capabilities: Configure handle positions and IDs for multi-channel workflow nodes
Set up edge styles including dashed connections and markerEnd settings
Implement NodeResizer with proper visibility gating and sizing constraints
Configure drag handles and nodrag or nowheel areas for interaction control
Integrate Markdown rendering with react-markdown and skipHtml safety
Prepare node components for Vercel AI SDK streaming integration
limitations: Does not generate complete React Flow canvas or application setup code
Cannot modify node behavior outside of React Flow standard API
Does not provide backend or API endpoint implementation
Limited to React Flow library version 11 conventions

use_cases.title: Custom Node Component Developer
use_cases.description: A frontend engineer building a workflow editor needs to create custom nodes with specific handle layouts and edge connections that follow React Flow semantics.
use_cases.target_user: Frontend developers working with React Flow

use_cases.title: UI Developer Debugging Node Interactions
use_cases.description: A UI developer troubleshooting drag, scroll, and resize conflicts in a React Flow canvas needs guidance on nodrag, nowheel, and dragHandle configuration.

use_cases.title: Integration Engineer Preparing for AI Streaming
use_cases.description: An engineer setting up node output components to receive streaming Markdown from an AI backend needs to prepare components without adding SDK dependencies prematurely.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Handle Configuration
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up source and target handles for a custom node
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to add source and target handles to a custom React Flow node. Use $workflow-node-setup to configure handle positions, IDs, and ensure edges reference matching handle IDs.

prompt_templates.title: Edge Styling Setup
prompt_templates.scenario: Styling edges with custom dash patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Use $workflow-node-setup to set up edge styles for my workflow. I need dashed connections with specific stroke colors and no arrow markers.

prompt_templates.title: Node Resize Configuration
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding resize capability to a custom node
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me add NodeResizer to my custom React Flow node. Configure it to be visible only when selected with minimum dimensions using $workflow-node-setup.

prompt_templates.title: Drag Handle and Interaction Zones
prompt_templates.scenario: Configuring drag handles and preventing unwanted interactions
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure drag handles, nodrag, and nowheel areas for a content-heavy workflow node using $workflow-node-setup.

output_examples.input: Add source handles to my custom workflow node
output_examples.output: A configured node with two source handles at right positions (top 85 and top 108 pixels), each with unique IDs that match the edge definitions in the flow model.

output_examples.input: Set up edge styles for my workflow
output_examples.output: A shared WORKFLOW_EDGE_STYLE constant with dashed stroke pattern using rgba color at 72 percent opacity, dash array 9-9, and 2 pixel width.

output_examples.input: Configure NodeResizer for my custom node
output_examples.output: A NodeResizer component added inside the custom node with selected-gated visibility, minimum height and width constraints, and custom handle and line style objects.

best_practices: Keep handle IDs synchronized between node definitions and edge references to prevent React Flow error 008.
Place NodeResizer as a direct child of the root node element, not inside clipped containers.
Prefer shared project primitives like WorkflowHandle over custom per-node styling for visual consistency.
anti_patterns: Do not store node dimensions directly in node components. Keep width, height, and position in the flow model.
Avoid placing handle elements inside overflow-hidden containers where they may be clipped by the parent boundary.
Do not render backend-provided HTML in workflow nodes. Always use skipHtml with react-markdown.

faq.question: What is React Flow?
faq.answer: React Flow is a library for building node-based editors and interactive workflow diagrams in React applications.

faq.question: Does this skill work with React Flow version 12?
faq.answer: This skill follows React Flow 11 conventions. Version 12 may have API differences for handles, edges, and NodeResizer.

faq.question: What is the NodeResizer component?
faq.answer: NodeResizer is a React Flow component that adds resize handles to custom nodes, allowing users to change node dimensions on the canvas.

faq.question: What does skipHtml do in react-markdown?
faq.answer: skipHtml prevents react-markdown from rendering any HTML tags found in Markdown content. This improves security against embedded HTML.

faq.question: Can this skill create a complete workflow editor?
faq.answer: No. This skill focuses on node-level configuration and debugging. Full canvas setup, state management, and backend integration are not covered.

faq.question: What is a dragHandle in React Flow?
faq.answer: dragHandle is a CSS selector that restricts node dragging to specific elements, such as a header bar, instead of the entire node surface.
